proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have a higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have a higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.723 on treatment_tamoxifen (n=15035) vs 4.680 off (n=34965); diff=+0.043 mo (Welch t-test).
Mean PFS 5.408 on treatment_palbociclib (n=17428) vs 4.310 off (n=32572); diff=+1.098 mo (Welch t-test).
Mean PFS 4.645 on treatment_trastuzumab (n=10039) vs 4.705 off (n=39961); diff=-0.060 mo (Welch t-test).
Mean PFS 4.660 on treatment_olaparib (n=4946) vs 4.696 off (n=45054); diff=-0.036 mo (Welch t-test).
Mean PFS 4.686 on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (n=4987) vs 4.694 off (n=45013); diff=-0.007 mo (Welch t-test).
Mean PFS 4.630 on treatment_pembrolizumab (n=7585) vs 4.704 off (n=42415); diff=-0.074 mo (Welch t-test).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In er_positive=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no tamoxifen (i.e., tamoxifen benefit is concentrated in ER+ disease).
In er_positive=0 patients, treatment_tamoxifen is not associated with higher mean pfs_months (tamoxifen has no benefit in ER- disease).
There is a positive treatment_tamoxifen × er_positive interaction in an OLS of pfs_months: the tamoxifen effect is more positive in ER+ than in ER- patients.

analyses.result_summary: In ER+ (n=34886): tamoxifen mean PFS 4.881 vs 4.858, diff=+0.023 mo.
In ER- (n=15114): tamoxifen mean PFS 4.354 vs 4.272, diff=+0.081 mo.
OLS interaction tamoxifen:er_positive coef=-0.058 mo, p=0.27.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no trastuzumab (HER2-targeted benefit in HER2+ disease).
In her2_positive=0 patients, treatment_trastuzumab is not associated with higher mean pfs_months.
There is a positive treatment_trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction in an OLS of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In HER2+ (n=9060): trastuzumab mean PFS 4.254 vs 4.334, diff=-0.079 mo.
In HER2- (n=40940): trastuzumab mean PFS 4.735 vs 4.786, diff=-0.051 mo.
OLS interaction trastuzumab:her2_positive coef=-0.028 mo, p=0.695.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no olaparib.
In BRCA1/2 wild-type patients, treatment_olaparib is not associated with higher mean pfs_months.
There is a positive treatment_olaparib × (brca1 OR brca2) interaction in an OLS of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In BRCA1/2 mutated (n=2499): olaparib mean PFS 4.933 vs 4.587, diff=+0.346 mo.
In BRCA wild-type (n=47501): olaparib mean PFS 4.646 vs 4.702, diff=-0.056 mo.
OLS interaction olaparib:brca_any coef=+0.402 mo, p=0.0208.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In her2_low=1 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no sacituzumab.
In her2_low=0 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is not associated with higher mean pfs_months.
There is a positive treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan × her2_low interaction in an OLS of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In HER2-low (n=18543): sacituzumab mean PFS 4.737 vs 4.768, diff=-0.031 mo.
In not HER2-low (n=31457): sacituzumab mean PFS 4.657 vs 4.650, diff=+0.007 mo.
OLS interaction sacituzumab:her2_low coef=-0.038 mo, p=0.626.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
In msi_high=0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab is not associated with higher mean pfs_months.
There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction in an OLS of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In MSI-high (n=498): pembrolizumab mean PFS 4.841 vs 4.701, diff=+0.141 mo.
In MSI-low/MSS (n=49502): pembrolizumab mean PFS 4.628 vs 4.704, diff=-0.077 mo.
OLS interaction pembrolizumab:msi_high coef=+0.217 mo, p=0.466.

Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower pfs_months.

Higher ki67_pct is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with lower pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with lower pfs_months (negative Spearman correlation).
Female (sex_female=1) patients have a different mean pfs_months than male patients.
Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (one-way ANOVA).
Black patients have lower mean pfs_months than white patients.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (one-way ANOVA).
Uninsured patients have lower mean pfs_months than privately insured patients.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have lower mean pfs_months than urban residents.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho(age_years, pfs_months) = +0.7264, p=0.
One-way ANOVA across race_ethnicity F=0.85 p=0.494; means white=4.679, asian 4.735, black 4.717, hispanic 4.707, other 4.762.
Black PFS 4.717 (n=5889) vs white 4.679 (n=32498); diff=+0.037 mo.
One-way ANOVA across insurance_type F=0.31 p=0.818; means medicaid=4.706, private=4.685, medicare=4.692, uninsured=4.733.
Uninsured PFS 4.733 (n=2046) vs private 4.685 (n=20090); diff=+0.048 mo.
Rural (n=9996) PFS 4.714 vs urban (n=40004) 4.688; diff=+0.027 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In postmenopausal=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no tamoxifen.
In postmenopausal=0 (premenopausal) patients, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no tamoxifen.
There is a non-zero treatment_tamoxifen × postmenopausal interaction in an OLS of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In postmenopausal (n=30238): tamoxifen mean PFS 4.702 vs 4.676, diff=+0.026 mo.
In premenopausal (n=19762): tamoxifen mean PFS 4.755 vs 4.686, diff=+0.069 mo.
OLS interaction tamoxifen:postmenopausal coef=-0.043 mo, p=0.392.

After multivariable adjustment, treatment_olaparib is independently associated with higher pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is independently associated with higher pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab is independently associated with higher pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (CDK4/6 benefit in ER+).
In er_positive=0 patients, treatment_palbociclib is not associated with higher mean pfs_months.
There is a positive treatment_palbociclib × er_positive interaction in an OLS of pfs_months.
In ER+/HER2- patients (er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0), treatment_palbociclib is associated with higher mean pfs_months than no palbociclib.

In ER+/HER2- (n=28589): palbociclib PFS 6.236 vs 4.312, diff=+1.924 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, albumin, LDH, NLR within the ER+/HER2- subgroup, treatment_palbociclib is independently associated with higher pfs_months.
After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, albumin, LDH, NLR within the HER2+ subgroup, treatment_trastuzumab is independently associated with higher pfs_months.
After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, albumin, LDH, NLR within the BRCA1/2-mutated subgroup, treatment_olaparib is independently associated with higher pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib effect in ER+/HER2- subgroup (n=28589): coef=+1.9304 mo, p=0.
Adjusted trastuzumab effect in HER2+ subgroup (n=9060): coef=+0.0044 mo, p=0.712.
Adjusted olaparib effect in BRCA1/2-mutated subgroup (n=2499): coef=+0.0887 mo, p=0.323.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving any biomarker-matched targeted therapy (tamoxifen+ER, palbociclib+ER+/HER2-, trastuzumab+HER2+, olaparib+BRCA1/2, sacituzumab+HER2-low/HER2-) have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving a biomarker-matched targeted therapy.
After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, albumin, LDH, and NLR, the biomarker-matched-treatment indicator remains independently associated with higher pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Patients receiving any biomarker-matched targeted therapy (n=20278) PFS 5.259 vs others (n=29722) 4.306; diff=+0.953 mo.
Adjusted biomarker-matched-tx effect (covariates: age, ECOG, stage IV, albumin, LDH, NLR): coef=+0.9528 mo, p=0.
